name: Ham Rolls with Grapes and Tomato Cream Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 400°F.
2. Melt 1 tbsp of butter in a saucepan and add the shallots. Cook briefly, then add the tomatoes. Cook about 10 minutes, stirring occasionally. Add 4 tbsp of the wine and cook for an additional 3 minutes. Stir in the cream and simmer for another 15 minutes.
3. Butter an oval baking dish (a dish measuring about 9 x 12 x 2 inches is ideal). Roll up the ham slices into cigarette shapes that are approximately 1-inch in diameter. Arrange the rolls close together in the dish, side by side in a neat row. Melt the remaining butter and brush over the ham rolls. Set aside.
4. Strain the sauce through a fine strainer and return it to the saucepan. Cook for an additional 5 minutes.
5. Meanwhile, place the grapes in a small skillet with the remaining wine. Bring to a boil and cook, shaking the skillet, for about 3 minutes. Do not overcook or the grapes will become mushy; they must retain their texture and shape. Add the sauce and bring to a boil, stirring.
6. Place the ham in the oven and bake for about 5 minutes, just to heat through.
7. Pour the grapes and the sauce over the ham.
